title: Dashboard shows when the next run is due
detail: Added a "next run ~HH:MM (in ~Xm)" indicator to the header so it is clear scrapes are hourly and not stalled — addresses the most common point of confusion when watching the timeline between runs.

title: Separated resilience from self-healing; added an integration section
detail: Reworked the Reliability section so in-run fault tolerance (Tor/provider rotation, boot check — inside the container) is distinct from self-healing (host-side recovery + the code-repair agent), with a clear 'what runs where' note. Added a 'Where this plugs in' section covering the integration points still being agreed and the env flags that switch features off without code changes.

title: Documentation + site staleness sweep
detail: Audited both repos, the READMEs, GitHub descriptions and the live site. Removed a false 'DOM fallback' parsing claim (it's JSON-LD only), deleted dead runner.sh + old launchd plist, rewrote the 3.6x7.gr README to the real tick.js/15-min + hourly-review mechanism with correct data shapes, and documented the captcha-capture env vars + compose mount.

title: Self-cleaning retention
detail: Added artifact retention to tick.js: keep ~72 runs / 60 captcha images, truncate the tick log, and docker image prune — so the repo and disk don't grow without bound.

title: Captcha capture + gallery
detail: Each solve attempt now saves a composite image (legend + photo + the solver's click overlay) and a verdict to a manifest, rendered as a live gallery on the site. First captured puzzle: legend [clock, megaphone, dinosaur], solved on attempt 2.

title: Tor exit-rotation was a silent no-op in the container
detail: The Dockerfile baked a control-port password hash that didn't match the plaintext the app sends, so every NEWNYM rotation failed auth (515) and a flagged exit could never be escaped. Fixed: hash generated at build time, rotateExit() now verifies the 250 OK reply, and the container self-checks auth at boot. Detected from the first failed run, diagnosed, patched, and pushed.
